sanskrit: अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः । सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥
hindiTranslation: अगर तू सब पापियोंसे भी अधिक पापी है, तो भी तू ज्ञानरूपी नौकाके द्वारा निःसन्देह सम्पूर्ण पापसमुद्रसे अच्छी तरह तर जायगा ॥
englishTranslation: Even if you are the worst of wrongdoers, you will cross all wrongdoing by the raft of knowledge alone.
essence: Clear understanding can carry even the heaviest past across.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Even if someone has done terrible things, clear understanding can still carry them beyond the whole burden of wrongdoing.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing your worst history does not have the final word.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen by guilt and confusion. Krishna keeps teaching him that knowledge changes everything: even someone burdened by the worst wrongdoing can cross over it. The next verse shows that this knowledge burns action itself.
modernRelevance: You replay the mistake in your head after the argument, the lie, the betrayal. Krishna says the mind can stop circling the wound: clear understanding can still carry you through.
translationLiteral: अपि (even) / चेदसि (even if you are) / पापेभ्यः (than wrongdoers) / सर्वेभ्यः (all) / पापकृत्तमः (most sinful) / सर्वम् (all) / ज्ञानप्लवेन (by the raft of knowledge) / एव (alone) / वृजिनम् (wrongdoing / trouble) / सन्तरिष्यसि (you will cross over)
insight: It shifts the mind from self-condemnation to transformation through clear seeing.

quoteVariants.text: No past is too heavy for clear understanding. Knowledge carries you across.
Even the worst past can be crossed by understanding.
